AQI Prediction Using Time Series – Kolkata (2019–2025)¶

  • Import Libraries
  • Load & Combine Dataset
  • Explore Data (EDA)
  • Handle Missing Values
  • Prepare Time Series
  • Train-Test Split
  • Model Training (ARIMA, SARIMA, Prophet)
  • Model Evaluation (MAE, MSE, RMSE, R²)
  • Prediction Example
  • Conclusion & Insights

1. Import Libraries¶

In [1]:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

from statsmodels.tsa.arima.model import ARIMA
from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller
from prophet import Prophet

from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, r2_score
In [ ]:

2. Load & Combine Dataset¶

In [2]:
MONTHS = ['January','February','March','April','May','June',
          'July','August','September','October','November','December']

records = []

for year in range(2019, 2026):
    df = pd.read_csv(f'AQI_daily_city_level_kolkata_{year}_kolkata_{year}.csv')
    df = df.rename(columns={df.columns[0]: 'Day'})

    # Keep only actual day rows (1 to 31)
    df = df[pd.to_numeric(df['Day'], errors='coerce').between(1, 31)].copy()
    df['Day'] = df['Day'].astype(int)

    # Wide format → Long format
    df_long = df.melt(id_vars='Day', value_vars=MONTHS, var_name='Month', value_name='AQI')
    df_long['Year'] = year
    df_long['Month_num'] = df_long['Month'].apply(lambda m: MONTHS.index(m) + 1)

    # Create proper Date column
    def to_date(row):
        try:
            return pd.Timestamp(int(row['Year']), int(row['Month_num']), int(row['Day']))
        except:
            return pd.NaT

    df_long['Date'] = df_long.apply(to_date, axis=1)
    df_long = df_long.dropna(subset=['Date'])
    records.append(df_long)

# Combine all years
df = pd.concat(records, ignore_index=True)
df = df.sort_values('Date').reset_index(drop=True)

In [7]:
# AQI Distribution
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
sns.histplot(df['AQI'].dropna(), kde=True, bins=20, color='steelblue')
plt.title('Distribution of AQI Values')
plt.xlabel('AQI')
plt.show()
In [8]:
# Average AQI by Month
monthly_avg = df.groupby('Month_num')['AQI'].mean()
month_labels = ['Jan','Feb','Mar','Apr','May','Jun','Jul','Aug','Sep','Oct','Nov','Dec']

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
plt.bar(month_labels, monthly_avg.values, color='steelblue')
plt.title('Average AQI by Month')
plt.xlabel('Month')
plt.ylabel('Average AQI')
plt.show()
In [9]:
# Yearly Average AQI
yearly_avg = df.groupby('Year')['AQI'].mean()

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
plt.plot(yearly_avg.index, yearly_avg.values, 'o-', color='purple')
plt.title('Yearly Average AQI Trend')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Average AQI')
plt.xticks(yearly_avg.index)
plt.show()
In [ ]:

4. Handle Missing Values¶

In [10]:
print('Missing AQI before:', df['AQI'].isnull().sum())

# Interpolate within each year-month group
df['AQI'] = df.groupby(['Year', 'Month'])['AQI'].transform(
    lambda s: s.interpolate(method='linear').ffill().bfill()
)

# Fill remaining gaps with monthly median
monthly_median = df.groupby(['Year', 'Month_num'])['AQI'].transform('median')
df['AQI'] = df['AQI'].fillna(monthly_median)

print('Missing AQI after :', df['AQI'].isnull().sum())

Missing AQI before: 279
Missing AQI after : 275
In [ ]:

5. Prepare Time Series¶

We aggregate daily AQI to monthly average — this makes ARIMA and SARIMA more stable and meaningful.

In [11]:
# Create monthly time series
ts = df.groupby(pd.Grouper(key='Date', freq='MS'))['AQI'].mean().dropna()
ts.index.freq = 'MS'

print('Monthly Time Series:')
print(ts)

Monthly Time Series:
Date
2019-01-01    342.903226
2019-02-01    250.678571
2019-03-01    161.451613
2019-04-01     80.733333
2019-05-01     84.225806
                 ...    
2024-11-01    158.866667
2024-12-01    158.612903
2025-01-01    171.645161
2025-02-01    117.250000
2025-03-01     94.258065
Freq: MS, Name: AQI, Length: 75, dtype: float64
In [12]:
# Plot the full time series
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))
plt.plot(ts.index, ts.values, color='steelblue', linewidth=1.5)
plt.title('Monthly Average AQI – Kolkata (2019–2025)')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('AQI')
plt.show()
In [13]:
# ADF Test – Check if series is Stationary
result = adfuller(ts)
print('ADF Statistic :', round(result[0], 4))
print('p-value       :', round(result[1], 4))
if result[1] < 0.05:
    print('Series is STATIONARY (good for ARIMA)')
else:
    print('Series is NOT stationary → differencing needed (d=1)')

ADF Statistic : -1.4921
p-value       : 0.5374
Series is NOT stationary → differencing needed (d=1)
In [14]:
# ACF and PACF plots to choose ARIMA parameters
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
plot_acf(ts,  lags=24, ax=axes[0], title='ACF Plot')
plot_pacf(ts, lags=24, ax=axes[1], title='PACF Plot')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [ ]:

6. Train-Test Split¶

In [15]:
# Train: 2019–2023  |  Test: 2024–2025
train = ts[ts.index < '2024-01-01']
test  = ts[ts.index >= '2024-01-01']

print('Train size:', len(train), 'months')
print('Test size :', len(test),  'months')

# Plot train/test split
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))
plt.plot(train.index, train.values, label='Train', color='steelblue')
plt.plot(test.index,  test.values,  label='Test',  color='orange')
plt.title('Train / Test Split')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('AQI')
plt.legend()
plt.show()

Train size: 60 months
Test size : 15 months
In [ ]:

7. Model Training¶

7a. ARIMA¶

In [16]:
# ARIMA(p, d, q)  →  p=2, d=1, q=2
arima_model = ARIMA(train, order=(2, 1, 2))
arima_fit   = arima_model.fit()

print(arima_fit.summary())

                               SARIMAX Results                                
==============================================================================
Dep. Variable:                    AQI   No. Observations:                   60
Model:                 ARIMA(2, 1, 2)   Log Likelihood                -290.525
Date:                Tue, 09 Jun 2026   AIC                            591.050
Time:                        13:18:39   BIC                            601.438
Sample:                    01-01-2019   HQIC                           595.105
                         - 12-01-2023                                         
Covariance Type:                  opg                                         
==============================================================================
                 coef    std err          z      P>|z|      [0.025      0.975]
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ar.L1          1.6743      0.055     30.190      0.000       1.566       1.783
ar.L2         -0.9442      0.051    -18.380      0.000      -1.045      -0.844
ma.L1         -1.7634      0.129    -13.659      0.000      -2.016      -1.510
ma.L2          0.7797      0.132      5.917      0.000       0.521       1.038
sigma2       997.8578    243.897      4.091      0.000     519.829    1475.886
===================================================================================
Ljung-Box (L1) (Q):                   1.78   Jarque-Bera (JB):                 1.67
Prob(Q):                              0.18   Prob(JB):                         0.43
Heteroskedasticity (H):               0.78   Skew:                             0.28
Prob(H) (two-sided):                  0.58   Kurtosis:                         2.40
===================================================================================

Warnings:
[1] Covariance matrix calculated using the outer product of gradients (complex-step).
In [17]:
# ARIMA Forecast
pred_arima = arima_fit.forecast(steps=len(test))
pred_arima.index = test.index

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))
plt.plot(train.index, train.values, label='Train',           color='steelblue')
plt.plot(test.index,  test.values,  label='Actual',          color='orange')
plt.plot(test.index,  pred_arima,   label='ARIMA Forecast',  color='red', linestyle='--')
plt.title('ARIMA – Actual vs Predicted')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('AQI')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [ ]:

7b. SARIMA¶

In [18]:
# SARIMA(p,d,q)(P,D,Q,s)  →  seasonal period s=12 months
sarima_model = SARIMAX(train, order=(1, 1, 1), seasonal_order=(1, 1, 1, 12))
sarima_fit   = sarima_model.fit(disp=False)

print(sarima_fit.summary())

                                     SARIMAX Results                                      
==========================================================================================

                                     - 12-01-2023

ar.S.L12      -0.8111      0.129     -6.280      0.000      -1.064      -0.558

In [19]:
# SARIMA Forecast
pred_sarima = sarima_fit.forecast(steps=len(test))
pred_sarima.index = test.index

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))
plt.plot(train.index, train.values, label='Train',            color='steelblue')
plt.plot(test.index,  test.values,  label='Actual',           color='orange')
plt.plot(test.index,  pred_sarima,  label='SARIMA Forecast',  color='green', linestyle='--')
plt.title('SARIMA – Actual vs Predicted')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('AQI')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [ ]:

7c. Prophet¶

In [20]:
# Prepare data in Prophet format (needs 'ds' and 'y' columns)
prophet_train = train.reset_index().rename(columns={'Date': 'ds', 'AQI': 'y'})

# Train Prophet model
prophet_model = Prophet(
    seasonality_mode='multiplicative',
    yearly_seasonality=True,
    weekly_seasonality=False,
    daily_seasonality=False
)
prophet_model.fit(prophet_train)

13:18:40 - cmdstanpy - INFO - Chain [1] start processing
13:18:41 - cmdstanpy - INFO - Chain [1] done processing
Out[20]:
<prophet.forecaster.Prophet at 0x28afd1986e0>
In [21]:
# Prophet Forecast
future   = prophet_model.make_future_dataframe(periods=len(test), freq='MS')
forecast = prophet_model.predict(future)

pred_prophet = forecast['yhat'].tail(len(test)).values

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))
plt.plot(train.index, train.values,  label='Train',             color='steelblue')
plt.plot(test.index,  test.values,   label='Actual',            color='orange')
plt.plot(test.index,  pred_prophet,  label='Prophet Forecast',  color='purple', linestyle='--')
plt.title('Prophet – Actual vs Predicted')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('AQI')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [22]:
# Prophet component plots (trend + seasonality)
prophet_model.plot_components(forecast)
plt.show()
In [ ]:

8. Model Evaluation (MAE, MSE, RMSE, R²)¶

In [23]:
def evaluate_model(y_true, y_pred, model_name):
    print(f'\n{model_name} Performance:')
    print('MAE  :', round(mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred), 4))
    print('MSE  :', round(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred), 4))
    print('RMSE :', round(np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred)), 4))
    print('R²   :', round(r2_score(y_true, y_pred), 4))
In [24]:
evaluate_model(test.values, pred_arima.values,  'ARIMA')
evaluate_model(test.values, pred_sarima.values, 'SARIMA')
evaluate_model(test.values, pred_prophet,       'Prophet')

ARIMA Performance:
MAE  : 21.8462
MSE  : 714.9045
RMSE : 26.7377
R²   : 0.7411

SARIMA Performance:
MAE  : 20.3226
MSE  : 672.6028
RMSE : 25.9346
R²   : 0.7564

Prophet Performance:
MAE  : 18.138
MSE  : 477.1739
RMSE : 21.8443
R²   : 0.8272
In [ ]:
In [25]:
# All 3 models on one chart
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
plt.plot(train.index, train.values, label='Train',            color='steelblue', linewidth=1.5)
plt.plot(test.index,  test.values,  label='Actual',           color='black',     linewidth=2)
plt.plot(test.index,  pred_arima,   label='ARIMA',            color='red',       linestyle='--')
plt.plot(test.index,  pred_sarima,  label='SARIMA',           color='green',     linestyle='--')
plt.plot(test.index,  pred_prophet, label='Prophet',          color='purple',    linestyle='--')
plt.title('AQI Forecast – All Models Comparison')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('Monthly Average AQI')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [26]:
# R² Score Comparison Bar Chart
model_names = ['ARIMA', 'SARIMA', 'Prophet']
r2_scores   = [
    r2_score(test.values, pred_arima.values),
    r2_score(test.values, pred_sarima.values),
    r2_score(test.values, pred_prophet)
]

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
bars = plt.bar(model_names, r2_scores, color=['red', 'green', 'purple'])
plt.title('R² Score Comparison – All Models')
plt.ylabel('R² Score')
for bar, val in zip(bars, r2_scores):
    plt.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
             bar.get_height() + 0.01, f'{val:.3f}', ha='center')
plt.show()
In [ ]:

9. Prediction Example – Forecast Next 6 Months¶

In [27]:
# Retrain SARIMA on full data and forecast next 6 months
sarima_full = SARIMAX(ts, order=(1, 1, 1), seasonal_order=(1, 1, 1, 12)).fit(disp=False)
forecast_6  = sarima_full.forecast(steps=6)

print('Next 6 Months AQI Forecast (SARIMA):')
print(forecast_6.round(2))

Next 6 Months AQI Forecast (SARIMA):
2025-04-01    91.58
2025-05-01    54.55
2025-06-01    46.51
2025-07-01    28.48
2025-08-01    50.31
2025-09-01    37.68
Freq: MS, Name: predicted_mean, dtype: float64
In [28]:
# Plot the future forecast
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))
plt.plot(ts.index,          ts.values,   label='Historical AQI',  color='steelblue')
plt.plot(forecast_6.index,  forecast_6,  label='6-Month Forecast', color='red',
         linestyle='--', marker='o')
plt.title('SARIMA – Next 6 Months AQI Forecast')
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('Monthly Average AQI')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [ ]:

10. Conclusion & Insights¶

In [29]:
print('Conclusion:')
print('1. Prophet performed best for AQI prediction due to strong yearly seasonality.')
print('2. SARIMA (seasonal=12) captures monthly seasonal patterns well.')
print('3. AQI is highest in winter months (Nov–Jan) and lowest in monsoon (Jun–Sep).')
print('4. Kolkata AQI shows a declining trend from 2019 to 2025 — air quality is improving.')
print('5. Time Series models are more suitable than standard ML for AQI forecasting.')
print('   because AQI depends heavily on time, season, and past values.')

Conclusion:
1. Prophet performed best for AQI prediction due to strong yearly seasonality.
2. SARIMA (seasonal=12) captures monthly seasonal patterns well.
3. AQI is highest in winter months (Nov–Jan) and lowest in monsoon (Jun–Sep).
4. Kolkata AQI shows a declining trend from 2019 to 2025 — air quality is improving.
5. Time Series models are more suitable than standard ML for AQI forecasting.
   because AQI depends heavily on time, season, and past values.
